improvement_directive: The note already clears the field floor. A re-derivation should keep the exact-covariance question separate and avoid wording the TV-envelope result as exact rollout-covariance optimality.
flagship_directive: Prove a matching lower bound for R_exact(beta,k,q) under the true monotone Bernoulli rollout covariance, showing that the Chebyshev-Lobatto schedule attains the same pointwise minimax base without passing through the TV-envelope surrogate.
critique: The delivered result is a genuine matched minimax rate theorem for the total-variation variance-envelope design problem: it characterizes the unbiased linear weights, reduces fixed-schedule risk to the l1 amplification A_beta(p), and proves matching lower and Chebyshev-Lobatto upper bounds for M_{beta,k,q} up to constants with base rho(q)/q. What is not proved is exact optimality for the true monotone nested-rollout covariance Gamma_P(p); the note only proves an envelope-to-exact upper bound and explicitly leaves exact nested minimax optimality open. The main mathematical engine is classical Chebyshev optimal recovery, so the novelty is the causal rollout design reduction and schedule optimization criterion, not a new approximation theorem. This is enough for a field-level contribution because the proved surrogate minimax is self-contained, nontrivial, and directly tied to rollout node placement, but it is not flagship because the exact covariance problem and any broader methodological breakthrough remain unresolved.
